description: Implemented the MDI patient-linkage gap between precheck and questionnaire bootstrap. After precheck, the intake UI collects transient demographics, POSTs them to /api/onboarding/mdi/patient, creates an MDI patient with retry-aware linkage/idempotency, stores only the MDI patient pointer locally, and sets a session-bound httpOnly questionnaire context cookie. /onboarding/mdi/bootstrap blocks precheck-complete users without an MDI patient id and redirects them back to /intake for patient profile collection. Questionnaire selection supports APOTH_MDI_QUESTIONNAIRE_IDS by treatment with fail-closed behavior when a configured mapping is missing. Follow-up fix: the deployed/serverless intake Lambda now mirrors the Next route by returning mdiPatientCsrfToken and mdiPatientLinked from /api/intake/bootstrap, returning mdiPatientCsrfToken from precheck success, and reading MDI linkage before deciding whether the user should proceed to MDI bootstrap. This fixes the deployed loop where /api/onboarding/mdi/bootstrap returned patient_profile_required because the patient-create step was skipped. Verified with focused infra Lambda/stack tests. Infra build still has unrelated ambient failures in existing Stripe typings and infra alias resolution.
title: Create MDI patient linkage before questionnaire bootstrap
